Close approaches

Earth approaches within ±15 days of the current date. Gold rings mark them in the scene; click to inspect.

Impact risk (Sentry)

Objects on the CNEOS Sentry risk list, ranked by cumulative Palermo scale. Click to inspect.

Close approaches to Earth

Click a row to jump time to that approach.

dot size ∝ brightness (H)
—
—

NEO Explorer

An interactive map of the inner solar system and all ~42,000 known near-Earth objects, positioned by propagating each body's real orbital elements (NASA/JPL data) for the current simulation time.

  • Drag to orbit, scroll to zoom, right-drag to pan.
  • Click any dot or planet to inspect it; Focus & follow locks the camera to it.
  • Space plays/pauses time; the bottom slider scrubs 1900–2100.
  • Filters narrows the swarm; Approaches lists Earth flybys near the current date; Risk shows the Sentry impact-risk list.
  • Share view copies a URL that restores this exact time, camera and selection.

Planet and Sun sizes are exaggerated (~500×) to be visible at solar-system scale; all positions and orbits are to scale in au. Ring labels on the ecliptic mark 1–5 au.
